Your Online Skin Doctor
caption:
Cystic acne is deep, painful, and doesn’t go away easily .
What helps:
• Benzoyl peroxide cleanser – reduces bacteria
• Adapalene (retinoid) – controls oil and inflammation
• Oral treatment – for severe or persistent acne
How to use:
Use benzoyl peroxide cleanser (leave 1–2 min, then rinse) .
Apply adapalene at night (start 2–3x/week) .
For severe cases, see a doctor for oral treatment .
Treat early to prevent scarring.

caption:
The COMPLETE protocol to safely erase Cystic Acne (Blind Pimples). 💣🆘

Squeezing a deep, cystic blind pimple (one with no whitehead) is the fastest way to cause a permanent ice-pick scar or hyperpigmentation. You need a targeted, multi-step attack to draw it out safely. 🧬

Here is the exact 5-step squad to fix it in order:

🔥 STEP 1 (WARM COMPRESS): Softens the hard tissue and draws the deep infection closer to the surface barrier.

💎 STEP 2 (MICRO-DARTS): The delivery system. Tiny, dissolvable darts create painless microscopic channels past the tough epidermis.

💚 STEP 3 (SALICYLIC ACID): The neutralizer. Travels down the new channels to dissolve the oil and fight the infection at the core.

🧽 STEP 4 (HYDROCOLLOID): The extractor. Once the infection is broken down, this patch safely absorbs the fluid to flatten the swelling.

💙 STEP 5 (CENTELLA/CICA): The peacekeeper. Cools down the angry post-blemish redness to prevent dark spots from forming.

📌 SAVE this exact order for your next skincare emergency and FOLLOW for more 3D skin science! 🐼

#cysticacne #blindpimple #acnetreatment #pimplepatch #salicylicacid

caption:
this was my skin before… acne, dark spots, rough texture, everything 😭

and if you’re dealing with this right now, I get it. it’s frustrating when nothing seems to work

but here’s what actually helped me clear my skin (and what can help you too):

first, understand your skin…
not every breakout is the same. acne, fungal acne, irritation, damaged barrier—they all need different treatments

second, stop overloading your skin
using too many actives at once will only make things worse. keep your routine simple and intentional

third, focus on these key ingredients:
• salicylic acid – clears pores and reduces breakouts
• niacinamide – controls oil + helps with dark spots
• azelaic acid / tranexamic acid – fades hyperpigmentation
• retinol (at night) – improves texture over time

fourth, repair your skin barrier
hydration is not optional. use gentle cleansers, moisturizers, and avoid harsh scrubs

fifth, sunscreen every single day
this is what protects your progress. without it, dark spots will keep coming back

and lastly… be consistent
clear skin is not overnight. it took time, patience, and understanding my skin

if your skin looks like my “before”, just know it can get better

save this as a reminder to stay consistent 🤍

creator_bio: Smarter skincare prescribed for you.
caption:
If your acne is hormonal, drying it out isn’t the answer.

Here are treatments that actually target hormonal acne:

1. Oral spironolactone Helps block androgen signaling from stimulating oil glands, which can reduce hormonal breakouts over time.

2. Topical spironolactone Works similarly, but applied directly to the skin to target oil production locally.

3. Winlevi (clascoterone) An FDA-approved topical anti-androgen that blocks hormones at the oil gland.

4. Isotretinoin (Accutane) The closest thing we have to long-term remission because it actually shrinks the oil glands, so the hormonal signal has much less “target” to act on.

At Honeydew, the goal isn’t just to dry out your skin - it’s to treat the root cause and give you the best chance at lasting control.

#hormonalacne #hormoneimbalance #spironolactone #accutane #dermpa

caption:
Tretinoin can cause some peeling in the first few weeks of using it, so I tell my patients to start low and slow. 

Low - Start with a low strength or something more mild, like a retinol or retinal
Slow - Start with only applying 2 - 3 times a week and only use at night on dry skin 

A little goes a long way, so be sure to only apply a pea sized amount to your face. 

Be sure to save this video for tips on how to apply later!

caption:
3 months done, 3 months to go with 50 daily mg of isotretinoin to take. I am melancholic over my unscarred and unbruised skin.

Yeah, my skin used to be pretty bad, with barely any clear spots on my face, but now I have to deal with the scariest hyperpigmentation of my life. All after the famous Accutane purge.

This is truly a miracle medication, as you can see through my video journal. However, it’s really not for the weak. Every time  I used to put on makeup to cover my cystic acne, I would pray for it to go away soon. 

I have three active pimples now. That’s a reason to celebrate! But I have a tough road ahead. I will be dealing with scarring and hyperpigmentation.

Wish me luck and please be kind in the comments❤️

creator_bio:
your fav derm | atx
skin science meets sisterhood 🎀
✎ teamdr.madalyn@gersh.com
caption:
what products or procedures have taken your skin to the next level?
 
you know you’re getting old when you’re flattered that your patients ask “are you old enough to be a doctor” 🥲 this is everything I did to preserve and maintain my skin in hopes of aging gracefully! 

- one thing I wish I did better was more aggressive spf use at an earlier age - prevention is everything, after all!

- retinoids helped with my acne early on, but have the added benefit of collagen stimulation - so that was an unwitting slay 🤭

- cleanser for a clean base, of course, but double cleansing really changed the game. 

- moisturizer to keep things hydrated because dry skin is dull, irritated, and/or wrinkled skin. you can often achieve a youthful plump with just moisturizing alone! this one is a must for me especially as a dry skin girly, but even oily skin needs moisturizer!

- adding an antioxidant was a game changer for skin vibrance - I personally love vitamin C but have been adding in additional antioxidants with each passing year lol. 

- my favorite add-ons to further stimulate collagen and combat fine lines recently have been peptides, growth factors, and exosomes! it has added a few steps to my routine so I sometimes skip these on busy days where I can only do the bare minimum basics

- as for procedures, I’ve shared all this before, but recently also tried sculptra and am starting to get more curious about skin tightening! these are all things that I recommend to my patients all the time because they’re things that I would personally do and/or would want for my mother, sister, father, or fiance (though dad and Evan have yet to agree to let me do anything for them 😒)

hashtag: skincareeducation, hibiclens, chlorhexidine, skintips, bodyodor, dermtok, hidradenitissuppurativa
caption:
PSA: Hibiclens is NOT daily skincare. 🚨
Hibiclens (chlorhexidine) is a surgical antiseptic cleanser used in medical settings before procedures. It’s not meant to be used as your everyday face or body wash.
Daily use can:
• Disrupt your skin barrier
• Alter your skin microbiome
• Cause dryness & irritation
The exception? Certain medical conditions like hidradenitis suppurativa, where doctors may recommend it.
Bottom line:
A cleanser that kills bacteria ≠ a healthy daily skincare routine.
#skincareeducation #hibiclens #chlorhexidine #skintips #bodyodor
#dermtok #hidradenitissuppurativa

creator_bio:
ER doctor telling stories
biz: drjoetiktok@gmail.com
caption:
Sunburn peeling might look satisfying, but it is actually your body removing skin cells that were damaged by ultraviolet radiation. When UV light injures the DNA inside skin cells, those cells can trigger a built in self destruct process called apoptosis. Your immune system then clears them away, which is why the top layer of skin starts peeling a few days after a bad sunburn. It is essentially your body getting rid of cells that were too genetically damaged to keep around.

#sunburn #skincancerprevention #dermatology #medicaleducation

hashtag: dermatologist, doctor, retinolburn, skincare, skintok, retinol, antiaging, dermreacts
caption:
Retinol burn occurs when the skin becomes irritated due to overuse or improper application of retinol-based products for your skin type. Using high concentrations of retinol or applying it too frequently can also increase the risk. 

To alleviate symptoms: allow your skin to heal by temporarily stopping the use of retinol, use a hydrating moisturizer, avoid harsh products that can further irritate the skin, protect your skin from sun exposure as retinol increases sun sensitivity. 

@lisat449

caption:
If your blackheads keep coming back no matter what you do… this is for you.
It’s not dirt.
It’s oil + dead skin clogging your pores every day .
So if you’re only scrubbing or squeezing it won’t fix it.
What actually helps:
• Benzoyl peroxide cleanser – reduces oil and bacteria
• Glycolic acid – clears buildup (1–2x/week)
• Niacinamide – controls oil daily
How to use, Keep it simple.
Cleanser at night,
Niacinamide daily,
Exfoliate only a few times a week.
Do this consistently → blackheads slowly stop coming back.

caption:
Niacinamide has loads of benefits depending on your skin type:

1. It decreases sebum production which helps the appearance of pore size— great for oily/acne prone skin
2. Helps increase ceramides which leads to decrease in TEWL (dryness)—great for dry skin
3. It is anti-inflammatory— great for acne/rosecea
4. It helps increase the production of collagen— anti-aging 
5. It helps decrease dyspigmentation by inhibiting melanosome transfer— improves hyperpigmentation 
6. Helps protect against UV and blue light

creator_bio:
Your skincare and life tips big sis
Not a doctor ✨
Newsletter on Substack 💻 

↓ skincare recs
caption:
I want to be completely clear:

My results have come from six years of using retinoids and three years of using azelaic acid. Most of my before and after pictures show the power of consistent, long terms use of retinoids. 

That being said, my skin really levelled up when I started using azelaic acid. I didn’t even start using it because I wanted more results, I was perfectly happy with my results. But I developed perioral dermatitis and azelaic acid is a treatment for that. My skin would certainly still have its glass quality without it, but azelaic acid is very soothing - something a retinoid absolutely isn’t!

So when I started getting little red splotches and flareups, yes the retinoid can help, but it has nothing on azelaic acid.

What you see in the first clip is irritation on holiday, and the subsequent clips are my skin on a more typical basis. Those types of flareups are non-existent for me now, and I do put most of that down to azelaic acid. I think your retinoid should act as your anti-acne, skin resurfacing an anti-aging heavy lifter. The AA helps with flareups, hyperpigmentation and evening out your skin tone. I put all my recommended products on my profile x #azelaicacid #skincare

caption:
Here’s some more info on the Skin 1004 Centella Ampoule. When it comes to extracts, the straight plant material is called centella asiatica extract but so is a pre-made formula that comes from any raw material supplier. 

Which means, with this serum, you are getting 100% of a pre made raw material formula that likely contains less than 5% of the actual centella asiatica plant. 

But this brand doesn’t tell you how much of the actual plant is in this formula. Nor do they tell you the extraction process, what part of the plant, or whether it is standardised in any of the active molecules that make centella so beneficial to your skin. 

I know a centella supplier that tests for these molecules and they have to reach 95% purity or the batch is rejected. Even 94.9% won’t make it through. So you know you’re getting these active components that actually impact your skin. 

If skin 1004 purchases this complex from a raw material supplier, they aren’t doing no R&D, no formulating, no innovation, just repackaging something that already exists, slapping 100% on it and selling it to you for a crazy mark up. 

Surely this is illegal right? 

Well in Australia it would be which is why I called it deceptive and shady. In Australia, if you get this from a raw material supplier and put it in your skincare, you need to list most of the ingredients in order of quantity. 

But in Korea you only have to list the name of the raw material complex.  

If this works for you and you like it, keep using it. You don’t actually need a lot of centella to see results. 

But apparently these Korean regulations are meant to be changing soon. #kbeautyskincare #beautyhacks #koreanskincare #kbeauty #skin1004 
#centella

caption:
Yes! You can be on spironolactone long term. Spironolactone is prescribed all the time by dermatologists to treat hormonal acne in women. While it is a relatively safe medicine, we recommend avoiding it during pregnancy and we recommend lab monitoring in certain patients based on their spironolactone dose, their other meds, their age, and their medical history.

creator_bio:
✨ Root-cause acne support for women
🧪 Functional labs & 1:1 virtual consults
↓ Learn more about my acne program ↓
caption:
Spironolactone works for acne because it lowers androgen activity. The real question is… why do so many women need that now?

It’s not that we all suddenly developed “acne genes.” It’s that modern physiology is constantly pushing androgen signaling up — even when labs look normal.

Chronic stress Blood sugar chaos Gut and liver overload A nervous system stuck in survival mode

All of these amplify oil production and inflammation at the skin.

So when you take spironolactone, oil goes down and breakouts calm. That doesn’t mean it fixed the root cause — it means the signal got muted.

And for many women, the moment they stop? The acne comes right back.

If the goal is lasting clear skin, you have to address why androgen signaling was loud in the first place.
That’s the part no one explains.

✨ and this is where a root cause approach to acne can change everything!

#spironolactone #acne #acnehealing

caption:
Beauty soup made with my favorite TCM beauty ingredients for natural plant collagen — all known to nourish the skin and balance the body from within. a dessert that won’t break you out OR make you bloat! 

Ingredients
A 1–2 tbsp dried peach gum — soak 12 hrs  
- 1 small tremella mushroom — soak 2–4 hrs, trim hard center  
- 3–5 red dates (jujube)  
- 1 tbsp goji berries
- 2–3 cups water
* Optional: rock sugar or honey to taste
* Optional: splash of milk or soy milk at the end

Instructions
1. Soak peach gum + tremella ahead of time.
2. Add all ingredients into a double-boiler pot.
3. Steam or simmer gently for 30–40 min until jelly-like.
4. Sweeten or add milk if desired.

Benefits ✨ Nourishes blood and yin ✨ Boosts skin elasticity (natural “plant collagen”) ✨ Brightens tired, dull skin
Enjoy warm or chilled — beauty from the inside out.

caption:
Eating your skincare is a real thing 😱 This is a classic TCM formula that your mom forces you to eat growing up🥣 It hydrates your skin from the inside out and is what makes that skin glowy and bouncy 🍩✨Take it every other day or once a week for the fall season 🍂🍁Too much of anything is not healthy for the body. We recommend making this in a batch. Remember to tag us when you try this! 🤳

RECIPE: Ingredients 🫚
- Snow fungus
- Red dates
- Gogi berries
- Rock sugar

RECIPE: Instructions 🥣

1. Soak the snow fungus in water for 1–2 hours until fully expanded and soft. Trim the yellow hard root portion and tear the fungus into small bite-size.

2. In a pot, bring 4–5 cups of water to a boil.

3. Add snow fungus and simmer on low heat for 1 hour or until the texture becomes soft and jelly-like. (The longer you simmer, the more gelatinous the snow fungus becomes. This is the collagen that helps with skin plumpness and moisture retention).

4. Add red dates and simmer for another hour.

5. Add goji berries and rock sugar and cook for 30 minutes.

6. Serve warm or chilled. For extra skin hydration, some people eat it daily or every other day, especially during dry seasons.

caption:
ad Skin longevity isn’t about chasing glow for a week. It’s about what you’re doing daily that supports your barrier, reduces inflammation and protects against environmental stress. 

If you’re exploring Ayurvedic skincare, here are a few things to look for:

•⁠  ⁠Ingredients that support the skin barrier (not just exfoliate it)

•⁠  ⁠Formulas rich in antioxidant botanicals to help counter oxidative stress

•⁠ Targeted formulas designed to help correct dark spots, improve uneven skin tone and restore visible radiance day after day

•⁠  ⁠A ritual you’ll actually enjoy and stick to

Ayurveda takes a long-term view of skin health, focusing on balance, resilience and supporting the skin’s natural function.

The Kumkumadi range from @Kama Ayurveda is rooted in that philosophy. It’s a botanical-led approach that combines radiance with barrier support, which is particularly helpful if you live in an urban environment where pollution and blue light exposure are daily realities.

If you’re curious about incorporating more natural, Ayurvedic principles into a modern routine, this could be a beautiful place to start.

As always, remember: no product replaces consistency, sun protection, good sleep and a healthy lifestyle. Skincare works best when it’s part of a bigger picture.

AD @kamaayurveda

caption:
Read caption 🫧🤍⤵️ 

Whew 😅 exactly a year ago i made a similar post but it was just the former part of this video and the caption was “Reminder: Acne doesn’t make you less pretty”
This post was a blessing and a curse at the same time, it blew up and alot of people came for me, some said really nasty things to me, some gave me a whole bunch of recommendations i didn’t ask for but despite everything i will always be grateful for one thing, the community i got from that simple post and i will do it over and over again.
I don’t say this enough but waking up to dm’s and comments of how i encourage some of you and how i give you guys hope hits deep and i am so grateful to God that i am able to do that for you guys. 
I still stand by the caption i made a year ago, ACNE DOESN’T MAKE YOU LESS PRETTY, it just makes you a pretty girl or boy with acne…that’s it! I will always be an acne girly, love it here! And to my acne community, this is a gentle reminder that a lot can change in a year, so keep going and don’t give up on yourself, you cannot! but while you are working on yourself and your skin be gentle with yourself and do it with grace. 
Love, kikie x 

#acnejourneytoclearskin #acnesurvivor #acnecommunity #acnepositivity #lynnekikie

creator_bio: holistic nutritionist certified in medicinal plants-this is not medical advice
caption: It really does matter how you take your Blackseed oil. Depending on your needs, this is going to differ. Every individual is different so it’s hard to say exactly what’s going to help someone the most so keep in mind that these are just some suggestions for dosages and timing. 1.) For High Blood Sugar or High Blood Pressure, it’s best to split the dose day and night to keep it steady in the system because blood sugar and blood pressure fluctuate throughout the day and the half life of thymoquinone plasma is about 4 to 6 hours depending. 2.) if you have auto immune conditions or have chronic inflammation, it is best to take it once daily about a half a teaspoon to 1 teaspoon per day— nighttime tends to be better for joint pain and inflammation as many inflammatory cytokines can spike at night. Taking it with food helps to make it more gentle on the system. 3.) if you have gut sensitivity or reflux making sure to take it with a little bit of food that has fat maybe a little bit of protein is the best approach so that it buffers the system and doesn’t hit so strongly. This can help you avoid the burping as well. Starting off with a lower dosage like a quarter of a teaspoon to a half a teaspoon once a day is good for starters. If you’re having an active flare, it might be best to avoid it until the flare calms consistency is also key. 4.)  for histamine issues or allergies once per day in the morning is most likely going to be best since histamines can be higher in the morning. It also covers you for your most reactive hours. It helps mass cells behave better so being consistent is ideal. You can split your dosage if you have postnasal drip or allergy issues at night as well. 5.) for perimenopausal or menopausal symptoms once per day is ideal during the day is best. If you have blood sugar crashes or if your energy is low and you have daytime inflammation it’s best to take it with a little bit of protein and fat during the day— however if your symptoms worsen at night, you can split your doses a quarter of a teaspoon to a half a teaspoon with a little bit of food. It is best to consult your doctor before using any herbal supplements, especially if you are pregnant, breast-feeding, or on medication. It is advised to take a break with Blackseed oil after three months since there aren’t any long-term studies to support longer-term use. Cycling offer for a few weeks and then back on is ideal This is not medical advice, and I am not a medical professional.

caption:
Struggling with white flakes and itchy scalp this winter?
l've got you - this natural hair mask is all you need!

This Multani Mitti + Buttermilk hair mask has been a total scalp saviour
🤍Multani Mitti - deeply cleanses the scalp, removes oil, dirt & buildup (main reason behind dandruff)
🤍Buttermilk - rich in lactic acid, gently exfoliates flakes & calms irritation while keeping scalp hydrated

✨I just mix 2 tbsp Multani Mitti with buttermilk, make a smooth paste & apply it from roots to ends
✨Keep it for 20-25 mins and then wash off with your regular shampoo - and that's it!
My scalp feels super fresh, no flakes, and hair feels soft, clean & bouncy even in this dry winter ❄️ 
Sometimes, the best fixes really are hidden in our kitchen

Would you try this DIY this winter?

📍Bedworth, UK
caption:
You’re still that girl 💅🏼🤍  

✨ SKIN TEXTURE IS NORMAL ✨
Acne, texture, & breakouts do not define you. They don’t make you any less beautiful, and they’re nothing to be ashamed of.

Your skin is allowed to have pores. It’s allowed to change. It’s allowed to go through things.

I see so many people apologising for their skin and you never need to. You’re welcome in my space exactly as you are — no judgement, just care, comfort and confidence building.

Healthy skin is real skin, and real skin has texture.
Be kind to yourself, babe. You’re doing better than you think. 

#skin #acne #makeupartist

hashtag: stitch, hyperpigmentation, darkspots, melasma, acnescars, discoloration, brightening, retinol, azelaicacid, dermatologist, shereeneidriss, dridriss, @ShereeneIdriss
caption:
#stitch with @Natseleen Top skincare picks for hyperpigmentation, dark spots, all types of brown discoloration as a board certified dermatologist who struggled with melasma 💥

It’s not a single ingredient story and you need more than just Vitamin C!

✅ Pigment Fighters: Alpha Arbutin, Licorice Root, Kojic Acid — ingredients that inhibit melanin production to brighten your skin @Dr. Idriss 

✅ Retinol: Helps with pigmentation by promoting skin cell turnover and reducing melanin production @The Ordinary 

✅ Azelaic Acid: Helps with pigmentation by inhibiting the enzyme tyrosinase, but also interferes with DNA synthesis in hyperactive melanocytes @Paula’s Choice 

✅ Sunscreen: Everything you’re doing is pretty much uselss if you’re not wearing sunscreen. The best one is the one you wear everyday!

#hyperpigmentation #darkspots #melasma #acnescars #discoloration #brightening #retinol #azelaicacid #dermatologist #shereeneidriss #dridriss

caption:
Stop trying to pop those tiny, itchy bumps! 🛑🍄

If you have clusters of tiny, uniform bumps on your chest, back, or forehead that itch and NEVER come to a whitehead... that is NOT normal acne. It is Fungal Acne (Malassezia Folliculitis). 🧬

Normal pimple creams will not work. In fact, heavy oils and thick creams actually feed the yeast and make it spread! You need a completely different approach. Here is your dermatologist-approved Fungal Rescue Squad:

🧼 STEP 1 (The Eradicator): Wash the area with an anti-fungal ingredient like Ketoconazole or Zinc Pyrithione (yes, the exact same stuff in anti-dandruff shampoo!) to stop the yeast from multiplying.
🧪 STEP 2 (The Unclogger): Salicylic Acid (BHA) is oil-soluble, meaning it dives into the infected follicle to melt away the trapped dead skin cells flattening the bumps.
💧 STEP 3 (The Safe Plump): Fungi feed on lipids (oils). You must repair your skin barrier using a 100% oil-free water gel hydrator so you don't accidentally feed the yeast again!

📌 SAVE this safe extraction routine, FOLLOW @skincaretheorydaily, and comment GUIDE to get our ultimate step-by-step Skincare Playbook sent right to your DMs! 🐼✨

#fungalacne #chestacne #tinybumps #texturedskin #skincarehacks

creator_bio: https://try-cura.app
caption:
How to kill fungal acne for good! Ketoconazole is an antifungal medication commonly used to treat “fungal acne,” which is actually a condition called Malassezia folliculitis. This condition is caused by an overgrowth of yeast (Malassezia) on the skin rather than bacteria. Ketoconazole works by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, specifically, it inhibits the production of ergosterol, a key component the fungus needs to survive. Without ergosterol, the fungal cells become weak, leaky, and eventually die, reducing the overgrowth that leads to itchy, acne-like bumps.

In terms of use, ketoconazole is often applied topically in the form of creams or shampoos (like 1–2% formulations). Even though it’s marketed as a dandruff treatment, the shampoo is frequently used on areas like the forehead, chest, or back! Step by step: you apply it to the affected skin, leave it on for about 3–5 minutes, then rinse, usually a few times per week. Consistency is key, as it helps keep the yeast population under control and prevents flare-ups. #fungalacne#ketaconazole#skincare#cura

creator_bio: Empowering you to take control of your health- Check out @philsmypharmacistfinds
caption:
Spending $40 on a body acne wash is criminal when this exists 👇
Body acne washes are a total racket. $30, $40, $50 for something you can make at home for $2.50 and it’ll actually be gentler on sensitive skin.
Here’s why this works: salicylic acid unclogs pores without stripping your skin dry. The trick is diluting it into a creamy, glycerin-rich base so you get the treatment without the irritation.
Here’s the exact recipe:
	∙	Dollar Tree 3% salicylic acid cream (full tube)
	∙	Simple baby shampoo (pour out until you have about 60g left)
	∙	Mix them together
That’s it. You end up with a 1% salicylic acid creamy body wash for $2.50 that does exactly what the expensive stuff promises.
Your skin doesn’t know the difference. Your wallet does. 💊

#philsmypharmacist #dollartreehacks #healtheducation #acnebodywash #bodyacne

caption:
Azelaic acid YES or NO? 
9 must-know facts.

🟢 Acne — YES (antibacterial + anti-inflammatory + helps unclog pores)

🟢 Dark spots — YES (helps with PIH + melasma by targeting excess melanin)

🔴 Wrinkles — NO (retinol is better for that)

🟢 Rosacea — YES (one of the few well-studied options)

🟢 Daily use — YES (start 1x/day → build up to 2x/day if tolerated)

🟡 Stinging at first — SOMETIMES (usually fades in minutes)

🟢 Purging — UNLIKELY (generally gentler than retinoids/BHA)

🟢 Sensitive skin — YES, with caution (introduce slowly)

✅ Would I recommend it? Absolutely.

Don’t layer it with retinoids or BHA at the same time—alternate and let your skin adjust first.

Pregnancy-safe.

Which one surprised you? 👇

caption:
Azelaic acid might not be as hyped as other skincare ingredients… but it should be 👀✨

This multitasking ingredient is amazing for:
✔️ Acne (it fights acne-causing bacteria)
✔️ Redness + rosacea (it calms inflammation)
✔️ Hyperpigmentation (it helps fade dark spots + post-acne marks)
✔️ Uneven skin tone (gives a brighter, smoother look)

What makes it special?
Unlike harsher actives, azelaic acid is generally gentle, making it perfect for sensitive or reactive skin 💗

It works by:
– Unclogging pores
– Reducing inflammation
– Slowing down excess pigment production

💡 Great for beginners or if your skin can’t tolerate strong acids or retinoids.

How to use it:
Start 2-3 times a week, apply after cleansing, and always follow with moisturizer + SPF ☀️

Consistency is key, results show over time, not overnight ⏳

caption: Let's talk about salicylic acid wash and how it can improve your skin! Salicylic acid is a type of beta hydroxy acid (BHA) that's commonly used in skincare products. It's known for its ability to exfoliate and unclog pores, making it a great ingredient for those with oily and acne-prone skin. Using a salicylic acid wash can help improve the appearance of your skin in a number of ways. It can: Clear away dead skin cells and unclog pores, which can reduce the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads. Reduce inflammation and redness, which can make pimples less noticeable and  elp control oil production, which can prevent future breakouts. Don't forget to follow up with a moisturizer to keep your skin hydrated!  So, if you're struggling with acne or oily skin, consider incorporating a salicylic acid wash into your routine. caption:
Most powerful ingredient, if used properly.💊 

Retinol/retinal is highly effective but can irritate.

Start with 2–3 times a week for the first 2 weeks, then build up to once daily.

✅ Reduces wrinkles — The most proven benefit. Period.

❌ Removes dark spots — Barely. Use TXA or Azelaic acid instead.

✅ Helps acne — Speeds up cell turnover. Not a cure, but it helps.

❌ Use in the morning — UV destroys retinol AND causes more irritation.

❌ Use multiple times a day — Once is enough. More = irritation.

✅ Use only a little — Pea-size to each needed spot. That's all.

✅ Can irritate your skin — Always pair with moisturizer.

❌ Safe for kids — No. Only for adults.

✅ Worth trying — One of the most effective ingredients out there.

Retinal is slightly stronger than retinol, but the same rules apply.

Which answer surprised you? 👇

caption:
TEXTURE IS NORMAL! 

And even w/ myself, yes I have nice skin [NOW], but I’ve battled w/ acne since a teenager & it seemed to get worse as I got older. Due to hormone changes & fluctuations, DIET, & stress levels. 

I am begging anyone who sees this to please stop comparing yourself to Influencers who use filters & very expensive skin treatments that everyday people may not know about, may be outside of their budget, etc. And that’s ok babe!

I, as a growing Content Creator & Makeup Artist w/ 20+ years of experience in beauty, do ALOT for my skin to look this way & I refuse to sell you a dream as if I don’t. This shxt takes a lot of work. And everyday clients/people don’t have time or disposable income to be buying hundreds & thousands on skincare. AND ITS OK!

So please be gentle with yourself, stop comparing, and know that it’s normal and you’re BEAUTIFUL babe! 

Love you!❤️

#skintexture #realskintexture #nofilterskin #losangelesmakeupartist #skincareexpert
